title: Years of Death and Rebirth Evas th anniversary
display_title: Years of Death and Rebirth Evas th anniversary
wikitext:
[]: ## Years of Death & Rebirth: Eva's ##th anniversary             Quote Post by V » Sun Oct 12, 2008 12:29 am Today marks the 13th anniversary of the premiere of the first episode of the Evangelion TV series in Japan, which occurred on October 12, 1995 amazing that a single anime series can be this popular after so many years; Gundam and such are different because they were multiple series (albeit not always set in the same continuity) and new ones come out all the time. And DragonBallZ or Pokemon and such just have hundreds of episodes. What other 26 episode series (plus a movie or two) has had such lasting impact? only a handful. Even Cowboy Bebop though, or Trigun, were not around for as many years as Evangelion was; Eva is older than those by a few years. Plus, no anime has remained so hotly debated as Evangelion. Cowboy Bebop is a classic but its not quite as thought-provoking as Eva (though I do enjoy the "near-Future" set design of both) alternative thread title: "It's been 13 years, where's the Live Action movie series?" and do you realize, that by next year, children who were not yet born when this series premiered, will be angsty 14 year olds whom the story still "speaks to"? Looking back, against all predictions a major remake of the series came out barely over a year ago with "Rebuild of Evangelion 1.0 : You Are (Not) Alone" in Japan, though it hasn't been released in North America nor has an English dub licensor been announced, and the second of four movies, "Rebuild of Evangelion 2.0 : You Can (Not) Advance" has been pushed back from a Novemberish premiere to an "early summer 2009" premiere. And Adult Swim stopped rerunning the series last year. Thus, we are still locked in anticipation for Evangelion to truly hit the North American fanbase as a whole. In the words of Hari Seldon, 13 years have been completed: 987 years of The Plan lay stretched out before you. Until the next Crisis, goodbye"

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sun Oct 12, 2008 12:50 am I guess all I can say is Congratulations! To who? I don't know. But the way I see we need to go out and reach people for Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Oct 12, 2008 1:00 am Wow, that means that the first episode of NGE premiered on the same day I first watched the last episode of Mahoromatic and irrevocably messed myself up. What are the odds of that happening? One in three hundred sixty-five.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Oct 12, 2008 1:08 am IrkenEvangelion wrote: I guess all I can say is Congratulations! To who? I don't know. But the way I see we need to go out and reach people for Evangelion.

[]: Re: 13 Years of Death & Rebirth: Eva's 13th anniversary             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sun Oct 12, 2008 1:16 am V wrote: Evangelion is exactly the same because it has multiple box sets and video games (albeit not always set in the same continuity) and new ones come out all the time.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Oct 12, 2008 1:19 am ....what about the new Director's Cut scenes and then End of Eva? do you realize how many people haven't seen those? and of course, it's my philosophy of "if it hasn't aired on North American television, it's still technically "new material" which really holds even for End of Eva in a sense. But seriously...I mean Renewal didn't come out THAT long ago....or rather yes, stuff from the original series did stop in 2003ish, but by then there were rumblings of live-action projects, and then word of Rebuild came out 2 years later so really there's always been an "Evangelion project" out in the "greater franchise", just not for the original series

[]:             Quote Post by Zaiku » Sun Oct 12, 2008 3:10 am I feel rather upset that I haven't been here for the early years, in fact I only got into it at the start of this year. But that just goes to show that people are STILL getting into Eva, and that it is spreading its fanbase. Thank you Anno, for these thirteen years of win.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Oct 12, 2008 11:59 am Update: wasn't October 12 it was October 4th; I was going by the site updates. Well, no one else made an anniversary thread before this.... (shrug)

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Oct 12, 2008 12:54 pm We had a 10th anniversary thread, but it would take some excavation.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Oct 12, 2008 2:04 pm Mr. Tines wrote: We had a 10th anniversary thread, but it would take some excavation.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Oct 12, 2008 2:12 pm By the by, where did this mysterious October 12th come from?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Oct 12, 2008 2:16 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: By the by, where did this mysterious October 12th come from?

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sun Oct 12, 2008 2:38 pm Of course, the 12th is the 13th Anniversary of the 2nd episode. At least its a date not pulled out of V's ass.

[]:             Quote Post by mrpanda » Sun Oct 12, 2008 2:54 pm CorporalChaos wrote: Of course, the 12th is the 13th Anniversary of the 2nd episode. At least its a date not pulled out of V's ass.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Oct 12, 2008 3:38 pm mrpanda wrote: CorporalChaos wrote: Of course, the 12th is the 13th Anniversary of the 2nd episode. At least its a date not pulled out of V's ass.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Oct 12, 2008 3:41 pm V wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: By the by, where did this mysterious October 12th come from?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Oct 12, 2008 5:28 pm oh srry; I think I was confusing it with the date of Second Impact which was the 13th. I honestly don't know now why I said 12th.

[]:             Quote Post by Zaiku » Mon Oct 13, 2008 5:03 am What I find exciting is that the twentieth anniversary is in 2015, we can make so many in-universe jokes about the third impact. Yes, we'll just have such a merry old time.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Mon Oct 13, 2008 5:42 am Zaiku wrote: What I find exciting is that the twentieth anniversary is in 2015, we can make so many in-universe jokes about the third impact. Yes, we'll just have such a merry old time.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Oct 13, 2008 9:36 am Zaiku wrote: What I find exciting is that the twentieth anniversary is in 2015, we can make so many in-universe jokes about the third impact. Yes, we'll just have such a merry old time.
